title: A/B serve models behind metric guardrails
description: Split traffic between model variants with predefined guardrail metrics and an automatic stop so a losing variant is bounded.
body: Use when comparing two model variants on live traffic rather than offline data. Do not run an open-ended split without guardrails — a worse variant silently harms the share of users routed to it. Actions: define the primary metric and guardrails upfront, route a small bounded share, and auto-stop the variant when a guardrail breaches; record exposure and outcomes per variant. Validation signals include a statistically powered comparison and a logged auto-stop that fired on guardrail breach. Risk boundary: an unbounded experiment on real users is an uncontrolled risk; if guardrails are absent, deny the split and require human approval of the exposure budget.
